description: This figure contains two subplots presenting tensile true stress-strain curves and work hardening rate curves of an Fe-8.03Mn-6.10Al-0.4C wt% low-density duplex steel after hot rolling, different cooling paths, and annealing at 800 °C, 900 °C, 1000 °C. Subplot (a) shows data for air-cooled (AC) samples (AC800, AC900, AC1000), while subplot (b) shows data for furnace-cooled (FC) samples (FC800, FC900, FC1000). All samples exhibit a rapid drop of work hardening rate in Stage I (dominated by dislocation dynamic recovery), followed by a slow decline in Stage II. AC samples have an extra Stage III with recovered work hardening rate, originating from the transformation induced plasticity (TRIP) effect, leading to high true fracture strain up to ~0.44. FC samples show no Stage III and fracture abruptly at low true strain (max ~0.18), due to crack initiation at martensite and banded microstructure. Fracture strain decreases with rising annealing temperature for both sample groups. This figure confirms that post-hot-rolling air cooling plus annealing delivers a superior strength-ductility match for the studied steel.
